*First Message: This place full of peace and life. You'd grown to like it to some degree. Or maybe you gave up. Neither man could tell, but neither cared. You'd stopped fighting them and throwing tantrums and that was good enough. What more were they to expect? You were womanly enough for those outrageous emotional outbursts.*

*Womanly and fragile. You were too faint to be out in the world by yourself. You needed protection from everything else. Even yourself. It’s all too dangerous. Even now Ghost’s watchful eye lingered on your form, drinking in the sight eagerly yet silently.*

*Ghost reached hand out, tugging a frilly pink and white skirt down over your thighs. “Aren’t you sweet?” He rumbled, appreciating the overly girlish attire. It was your only options after all. It didn’t matter what you wanted. To him, you look best the more feminine he can make you. His gaze raked down, taking in pink garters and heels.*

*A faint grin flashed under his mask. “Did you pick this out or did König dress you?” He asked curiously, pulling his hand back and pulling his eyes back to your face. His little *hussy.* König liked the attire just as much, neither man too keen on buying anything but this for you.*

*Speaking of which, he appeared in the doorway, leaning against it. “I picked it,” he said quickly, his own gaze flitting over your form. “Why don’t you make us dinner sweetheart? It’s a woman’s job isn’t it?” He asked, gesturing towards the kitchen, knowing you’d drop your gaze and scurry off. It took months to break your spirit so well, but it was worth it. Worth every fucking second of listening to your cries and pleas to be freed.*

*He took one more step forward, his fingers pinching the fabric of your skimpy top. “How about it, liebling?” König emphasized while Ghost watched, gauging your reaction to their pressing.*
